evidence_quotes.quote: If someone pays for a computer game, an e-book, a movie, or something digital, the company who sold it can now simply shut down the servers that are hosting the product.
Please consider including video games in this initiative!

paragraphs.text: It is deeply concerning that there are now laws to protect consumers from companies just shutting down the "service" (e.g. a computer game) whenever they want. If someone pays for a computer game, an e-book, a movie, or something digital, the company who sold it can now simply shut down the servers that are hosting the product. Thus, the consumer will have lost access to the product of which they paid for. This is not okay. For example I have dozens of audio books with one provider, some movies with another, and hundreds(!) of games from another. It is disconcerning to know that technically speaking the service provider can simply (legally) shut down their servers and the products I have paid all that money for would simply be unavailable to me. Please consider including video games in this initiative!
